reason: tests need rewrite, not instruction — remaining rubric failures (solution_uniqueness_guard, tests_verify_behavior_not_text, test_not_tautological, behavior_in_tests) are test-quality issues: the f2p tests assert exact string matches (createRafWithFallbackTimer, called, cancelAnimationFrame, etc.) rather than exercising runtime behavior. No instruction revision can pass these without leaking the solution. The two instruction-related failures (no_solution_leakage, instruction_no_hint_leakage) are fixed by removing the 'Implementation Requirements' section that dictated exact code.
